name: Environment Variable Management
description: Manage environment variables securely with dotenv, validation, and type-safe configuration for Node.js applications.

steps.title: Install and configure dotenv
steps.description: Set up dotenv to load environment variables from a .env file during development.
steps.command:
npm install dotenv
echo 'require("dotenv").config();' | cat - app.js > /tmp/app.js && mv /tmp/app.js app.js
steps.tip: Load dotenv as early as possible in your entry file, before importing any modules that use env vars.

steps.title: Create .env and .env.example files
steps.description: Create the environment file for local development and a template for team onboarding.
steps.command:
cat <<'EOF' > .env
NODE_ENV=development
PORT=3000
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://localhost:5432/mydb
JWT_SECRET=dev-secret-change-in-production
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
EOF

# Create template without secret values
sed 's/=.*/=/' .env > .env.example
steps.warning: Never commit .env to version control. Always commit .env.example as a template for other developers.

steps.title: Add .env to .gitignore
steps.description: Ensure the .env file with real values is never committed to the repository.

steps.title: Validate environment variables at startup
steps.description: Add runtime validation to fail fast if required environment variables are missing.
steps.tip: Parse numbers and booleans from env vars immediately in the config module. Never parse process.env values inline throughout your code.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Node.js Environment Variable Setup

echo "==> Installing dotenv..."
npm install dotenv 2>/dev/null || echo "dotenv already installed"

echo "==> Creating .env file..."
if [ ! -f .env ]; then
  cat > .env <<'EOF'
NODE_ENV=development
PORT=3000
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://localhost:5432/mydb
JWT_SECRET=change-this-in-production
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379
LOG_LEVEL=debug
EOF
  echo "  Created .env"
else
  echo "  .env already exists, skipping"
fi

echo "==> Creating .env.example..."
sed 's/=.*/=/' .env > .env.example
echo "  Created .env.example"

echo "==> Updating .gitignore..."
for PATTERN in ".env" ".env.local" ".env.production"; do
  if ! grep -q "^${PATTERN}$" .gitignore 2>/dev/null; then
    echo "${PATTERN}" >> .gitignore
    echo "  Added ${PATTERN} to .gitignore"
  fi
done

echo "==> Creating config module..."
cat > config.js <<'EOF'
require('dotenv').config();

const required = ['DATABASE_URL', 'JWT_SECRET'];
const missing = required.filter(key => !process.env[key]);

if (missing.length > 0) {
  console.error('FATAL: Missing required env vars:', missing.join(', '));
  console.error('Copy .env.example to .env and fill in values.');
  process.exit(1);
}

module.exports = {
  port: parseInt(process.env.PORT || '3000', 10),
  nodeEnv: process.env.NODE_ENV || 'development',
  databaseUrl: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
  jwtSecret: process.env.JWT_SECRET,
  redisUrl: process.env.REDIS_URL || 'redis://localhost:6379',
  logLevel: process.env.LOG_LEVEL || 'info',
  isDev: process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production',
  isProd: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production'
};
EOF

echo ""
echo "==> Setup complete. Usage:"
echo "  const config = require('./config');"
echo "  console.log(config.port); // 3000"

faq.question: Should I use dotenv in production?
faq.answer: Generally no. In production, set environment variables through your deployment platform (Docker, Kubernetes, AWS, etc.). Dotenv is primarily for local development convenience.

faq.question: What is the difference between .env and .env.local?
faq.answer: By convention, .env contains defaults, .env.local contains overrides for your local machine. Frameworks like Next.js and Vite support this hierarchy automatically, loading .env.local with higher priority.

faq.question: How do I use different env files per environment?
faq.answer: Create .env.development, .env.staging, .env.production files and load the right one: require('dotenv').config({ path: `.env.${process.env.NODE_ENV}` }). Most frameworks handle this automatically.
